flashcards.back: The process of breaking down a raw, unstructured log entry into its constituent, meaningful parts or fields (e.g., timestamp, source IP, event ID).

The process of converting parsed log fields into a standardized, common format using consistent field names, data types, and values (e.g., mapping all source IP fields to `src_ip`, converting all timestamps to UTC).

flashcards.front: What is the primary technical tool used for parsing unstructured text logs?
Why is normalization essential for effective SIEM correlation?
flashcards.back: It allows the correlation engine to compare the same type of data (e.g., source IP) across different log sources by ensuring they are stored under a common field name (e.g., `src_ip`).

flashcards.front: Name two common challenges in log parsing and normalization.
flashcards.back: 1. Dealing with custom application log formats. 2. Managing the performance overhead of parsing large volumes of data in real-time.
